title: Living in Boulder blog 8 - 28 - 2017: Strava Segment Regording. ? Miles on Rollers.

enrichment.summary: Kyle gives a casual Tuesday morning life update from Boulder, covering why he skipped the previous night's log (working late), his car making a buzzing noise he needs the dealership to fix, and his day plan to ride his bike. He shares his habit of reading audiobooks synced with text on a big TV while riding rollers, and his excitement about adding Wikipedia photos to his digital books to make them easier to follow.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: loves syncing audiobook playback with scrolling text on a big screen — keeps him focused and prevents mind-wandering
strongly prefers books with images; excited about inserting Wikipedia photos into his ebooks to aid memory and navigation
almost prefers annotating books over making and converting videos despite the extra effort

claims.subject: Kyle

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Yesterday I was doing videos of mountain biking.

claims.subject: Kyle

claims.subject: Kyle

claims.claim: The book reads to you and you just read along with it on fast speed, about 50 seconds per page.

claims.subject: Kyle

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: It would be a lot better if the book had pictures.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I stopped running my bike not because I had to get up early or was tired, but because I wanted to put photos on my old books.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I have photos on Google that I got from Wikipedia on my Flickr.

claims.subject: Kyle

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Having pictures on either side would make it easier to mark where you are in the book.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: The photos would probably look really cool on a big screen.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I couldn't attach photos while the converter was running.

claims.subject: Kyle

claims.subject: Kyle

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm going to sober up pretty soon because I only have a few days before more speaking videos.

transcript.text: Hello, living in Boulder V-Log. It's Tuesday at 11.30 a.m. I didn't do a V-Log last night because I was working too late. I was writing until like 2 or something and then I was doing something on my computer and I can't remember what it was.

But then I just got too tired because I wanted to get up today. I needed to take my car to the shop to have the guy look at it. It's got this buzz going on all the time and it's really annoying and we don't know how to stop it. So he told me to ground the wire but it didn't do anything. So he's going to look at it next week.
Luckily it's a mechanic who used to work at the factory so I think I'll be able to figure it out.

Or the dealership. Anyway, yeah, so yeah, today I got all day to ride so it's nice. I just came back from my mom's house. I was cleaning her house and she wanted me to have my car parked over there for some reason.
But, uh, so, uh, yeah, I'm just going to ride. Yesterday I was doing the videos of mountain biking and, uh, yeah, I think I'm just on Google Earth and I think I'm going to continue with that.
Um, and, uh, listened to, uh, Plato. I went all the way through, man, I can't remember. I went through all the way through a book yesterday and I was reading it along, um, while I ride the bike.
I'm looking at my big TV on the wall. I'll do a demo later. But, uh, you read, it reads to you and you just read along with it.
Um, on the fast speed gets about a page of 50 seconds for a page. And, uh, then I just scroll down. I have the table right in front of me, so I just scroll. It's pretty cool.
Um, it, it really helps, uh, I'm following along without spacing out and painting, like thinking about something else. Um, but it would be a lot better if it had pictures.
Oh, that's what I was doing. I just remembered what I was doing last time. Um, yeah, I'm riding my bike. Microfine in. Um, I'm riding my bike and I find, I stopped riding my bike.
You know, it's like two in the morning, stopped riding my bike, not because I had to get up early in the next day or because I was tired, but because I wanted to put photos on my, uh, old books.

I have all these photos on Google that I got from Wikipedia on my Flickr and, uh, I want to, uh, attach them because when I was reading the books, I kept thinking to myself, uh, this would be so much easier to remember, especially if you're not, if you're just, you know,
Scrolling down and, um, like looking for stuff, it's so much easier to mark where you are in the book, um, along with the scroll on the right.
If you had pictures on either side, having to do with what you're reading and I have the space there. So, um, and yeah, they'd probably look really cool on a big screen anyway.

Um, so, uh, I wanted to do that, but I couldn't do it while the converter was gone. It was converting. Um, but when it stopped converting, that's when I got off the bike and I, uh, yeah, I lost my train of thought.
It was the thing I was doing last night. Oh yeah. The photos. So yeah, um, I can, I can zoom those photos in really fast.

I mean, put those photos in on the pages really fast. So I was experimenting with that. It was really exciting. I was like just getting started. So I almost feel like I would, might prefer to do that even then making videos and converting them, even though it's harder.
But, cause, uh, yeah. So anyway, um, I just remembered. So I guess I'll do that and then I'll come back and I'll ride my bike.
So anyways, I've babbled for long enough. I'm going to sober up pretty soon. I only have a few days of weed more left and then I'll, I'll do lots of speaking videos. Okay. Peace.
